methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Solideon operates in Robotics & Manufacturing at the Pre-Seed stage, having raised $6.5M to date. The valuation landscape comprises one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, though none qualify as sufficiently tight comparables at the Pre-Seed stage.

Given the absence of a robust same-stage peer set in the sector, stage-progression methodology provides the primary analytical anchor. Using total capital raised ($6.5M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Pre-Seed to next-stage advancement yields $16.25M. This method reflects market practice for the progression from capital infusion to institutional validation, though it relies on the assumption that raised capital approximates fair valuation at entry.

The triangulation supports a range of $14M to $19M, centered at $16M. The narrow methodological basis—one applicable approach rather than multiple independent techniques—introduces material uncertainty. Key unknowns include Solideon's revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and technology differentiation versus the identified private comparables. A peer-median cross-check remains infeasible without additional Pre-Seed or early Seed stage datapoints. Confidence in this range improves materially upon acquisition of operating metrics or a tightened peer cohort at adjacent valuation tiers.
